mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that outpaces supply constraints, driving sustained corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle indicates capacity utilization bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO trends during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 2.5% annual threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales remain flat, signals a structural shift in consumer demand away from durable goods, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a period of elevated inflation indicates successful supply chain normalization and margin expansion, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO rebounds during high inflation periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.
